articles.title: ROLLER Partners with Adyen to Offer Embedded Financing to 3,000+ Venues
articles.summary: ROLLER has partnered with Adyen to launch an embedded financing solution that gives FEC operators fast access to capital directly through their venue management platform. The integration eliminates the need for traditional bank lending processes, allowing operators to fund expansions, equipment purchases, or cash flow needs more quickly. This is a significant development for the thousands of attractions and FEC venues already using ROLLER as their core operating system.

articles.title: Periscope Equity Makes Strategic Investment in Amusement Connect
articles.summary: Private equity firm Periscope has made a strategic investment in Amusement Connect, a platform serving the amusement and FEC industry. The infusion of institutional capital signals growing investor confidence in amusement technology and FEC-focused software solutions. Operators should watch how this funding shapes Amusement Connect's product development and market reach.

articles.title: Periscope Equity Confirms Investment in Amusement Connect Platform
articles.summary: Confirming earlier reports, Periscope Equity has formally announced its strategic investment in Amusement Connect, underlining private equity's increasing interest in FEC-adjacent technology businesses. The deal highlights how amusement industry software is attracting outside capital. FEC operators and suppliers should note the potential for accelerated product innovation at Amusement Connect.

articles.title: GrowthPro Group Launches Summer 2026 Education Series for FEC Operators
articles.summary: The GrowthPro Group has announced a summer lineup of Peer Talks and webinars tailored specifically for family entertainment centers, bowling centers, and attractions operators. The series opens June 11 with a session on artificial intelligence in the entertainment industry. FEC operators looking to stay current on AI applications and peer-driven best practices should consider registering.

articles.title: Sacoa Installs Cashless Payment Technology at Virginia Pinstack Location
articles.summary: Sacoa has completed a cashless technology installation at a Pinstack location in Virginia, continuing the industry trend toward fully integrated card and digital payment systems in bowling and FEC venues. Cashless systems are proven to increase per-guest spend and streamline operations. This deployment offers a practical case study for operators evaluating similar upgrades.

articles.title: SisyFox Releases FoxBall 6.0 with New Subscription Licensing and Performance Upgrades
articles.summary: SisyFox has launched FoxBall 6.0 featuring major performance improvements and a new flexible licensing model that allows operators to choose between lifetime and subscription plans. The subscription option lowers the upfront investment barrier for FEC operators wanting to add active gaming attractions. This model shift could make interactive physical gaming experiences more accessible to smaller venues.

articles.title: Querion Hypermedia Entertainment Park Opens to Sold-Out Crowds in Poland
articles.summary: Querion, a new all-weather hypermedia entertainment park in Piechowice, Poland developed by Attraktion! and hotel operator Górskie Resorty, opened to a sold-out weekend. The venue is designed as a year-round destination for families, tourists, and event groups in a mountain resort region. The sold-out opening demonstrates strong demand for immersive indoor entertainment anchors within resort and hotel developments.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Park Continues Nationwide Expansion with New Maryland Location
articles.summary: Urban Air is pushing forward with its national growth strategy, adding a new indoor entertainment venue in Maryland to its expanding franchise footprint. The chain continues to be one of the most aggressive FEC franchise concepts in the U.S. market. Operators and investors tracking the trampoline park and adventure park sector should monitor Urban Air's pace of openings.

articles.title: New Urban Air Adventure Park Opens in Salem, Oregon
articles.summary: A new Urban Air Adventure Park has opened in Salem, offering a preview of the brand's indoor family entertainment format in the Pacific Northwest market. The opening adds another data point to Urban Air's ongoing national franchise expansion. Local FEC competitors should take note of the arrival of a well-capitalized national chain in the market.

articles.title: Inside Salem's New Urban Air Adventure Park: A Tour of the Facility
articles.summary: A walkthrough of the newly opened Urban Air Adventure Park in Salem provides a look at the attraction mix, layout, and design choices made for this new location. The tour highlights the variety of active play zones that Urban Air deploys to drive dwell time and repeat visits. FEC operators can use this as a reference point for benchmarking their own attraction offerings.

articles.title: ROLLER and Adyen Launch Embedded Financing Solution for Venue Operators
articles.summary: ROLLER's new embedded financing product, built in partnership with payment giant Adyen, allows FEC and attractions operators to access growth capital directly within their venue management software. The streamlined lending process is designed to reduce friction and time-to-funding compared to traditional financing routes. This product addresses a longstanding pain point for independent and multi-location FEC operators seeking flexible capital.

articles.title: Plymouth, UK to Get Large-Scale Go-Kart and Laser Tag Entertainment Hub
articles.summary: A new large-format entertainment venue featuring go-karting and laser tag is planned for Plymouth, England, representing a significant addition to the UK's FEC market. The project reflects continued investor appetite for multi-attraction indoor entertainment destinations in secondary UK cities. FEC developers and suppliers eyeing the UK market should note this as a new competitive venue in the Southwest.

articles.title: Tenpin Opens New Entertainment Centre in Blackpool
articles.summary: Tenpin has opened a new entertainment centre in Blackpool, expanding its UK bowling and family entertainment footprint to a high-footfall coastal leisure destination. The opening underlines Tenpin's continued brick-and-mortar growth strategy in the UK market. Operators watching the UK bowling and FEC sector will find this opening relevant for competitive benchmarking.

articles.title: Madison, Mississippi Entertainment District 'The Gallery' to Break Ground This Summer
articles.summary: A new mixed-use entertainment district called The Gallery is set to break ground in Madison, Mississippi this summer, signaling continued investment in suburban entertainment development in the South. Projects like this often anchor FEC tenants and dining concepts within a walkable retail environment. Suppliers and operators looking for new venue opportunities should monitor this development.

articles.title: Indoor Entertainment Center Leases Former Sears Space at Sunrise Mall
articles.summary: An indoor entertainment center has signed a lease for part of the former Sears anchor space at Sunrise Mall, continuing the well-documented trend of FEC concepts backfilling dying department store square footage. The adaptive reuse of large-format retail space remains one of the primary real estate strategies for expanding FEC brands. This deal is a useful case study for operators and developers evaluating similar opportunities.

articles.title: Spokane Valley Family Entertainment Center Closes After Two Years in Business
articles.summary: A family entertainment center in Spokane Valley, Washington has permanently shut its doors after just two years of operation, highlighting the operational challenges facing independent FEC operators. The closure comes ahead of summer, typically the strongest revenue period for the sector. Industry observers should consider this a reminder of the importance of robust business planning, capitalization, and market analysis before opening.

articles.title: Six Flags Expands Flexible Membership Program Across More Parks
articles.summary: Six Flags is rolling out its flexible membership program to additional parks in its portfolio, offering guests more customizable access options in a bid to grow recurring revenue. Flexible membership and subscription models are increasingly being adopted across the FEC and attractions industry as operators seek predictable income streams. Independent FEC operators may find the Six Flags approach instructive for their own loyalty and membership program design.

articles.title: Legoland Invests in Family Entertainment Growth as the Sector Thrives
articles.summary: Legoland is committing capital to growth as the broader family entertainment sector demonstrates strong consumer demand. The investment signals that major branded FEC operators see continued opportunity in the family attractions market. Operators across the FEC spectrum can take confidence from this macro-level signal of sector health.

articles.title: Brogent Technologies Opens Advanced R&D and Manufacturing Facility in Taiwan
articles.summary: Brogent Technologies has inaugurated a new facility at Ciaotou Science Park in Kaohsiung, Taiwan, expanding its capacity for research, development, and manufacturing of immersive ride systems. The facility strengthens Brogent's end-to-end production capabilities for the global attractions industry. FEC operators and theme park buyers interested in motion-based ride technology should note Brogent's increased production capacity.

articles.title: WildBrain CPLG to Lead Location-Based Entertainment Licensing for Miraculous Across EMEA and APAC
articles.summary: WildBrain CPLG has been appointed to drive location-based entertainment opportunities for the Miraculous franchise outside the Americas under a new multi-year agreement. The deal opens pathways for FEC operators in Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and Asia-Pacific to license Miraculous-branded experiences. Operators seeking popular IP to anchor themed zones or immersive attractions should explore what this licensing expansion means for their region.

articles.title: RePlay Magazine Publishes Party Professor Column for June 2026
articles.summary: RePlay Magazine's June 2026 Party Professor column delivers practical guidance for FEC and amusement operators managing party bookings, group events, and birthday business. Party and group events remain among the highest-margin revenue streams for most FEC venues. Operators should review this column for actionable tips on maximizing event revenue during the summer peak season.

articles.title: Three Women Charged After Riot Forces Urban Air in Raleigh to Close Early
articles.summary: A disturbance at an Urban Air location in Raleigh, North Carolina led to three arrests and an early closure of the facility, drawing local media attention to safety and crowd management challenges at family entertainment venues. Incidents like this underscore the importance of proactive security protocols, staff training, and clear code-of-conduct enforcement at FEC locations. Operators should use this as a prompt to review their own security and incident response plans.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Partners with Toy Foundation for International Day of Play Promotion
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese is offering free Adventure Zone admission in partnership with the Toy Foundation to celebrate International Day of Play, while running a June fundraiser. Cause-related marketing tie-ins and community-focused promotions are effective tools for FEC operators to drive traffic and build brand goodwill. The campaign offers a template for how FEC brands can align with nationally recognized events to generate earned media and foot traffic.

articles.title: NRG Adventure Park Progresses at Southside Commons in Jacksonville
articles.summary: NRG Adventure Park is advancing its development at Southside Commons in Jacksonville, Florida, adding to the pipeline of new FEC venues opening in the Southeast. The project represents a new entrant competing in one of the country's more active FEC markets. Operators and suppliers in the Florida market should take note of this incoming competitor and the mixed-use retail context in which it is being developed.

articles.title: London-Style Interactive Entertainment Venue with Virtual Soccer and Games Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A new indoor entertainment concept originating from London, featuring virtual soccer, foosball, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food, is set to open in Paris. The concept blends social gaming with food and beverage in a format that is gaining traction across European urban markets. FEC operators exploring social entertainment or bar-arcade hybrid models should follow this concept's reception in the Paris market.

articles.title: Menasha's Sabre Lanes Closes, Directs Customers to Appleton Bowling Center
articles.summary: Sabre Lanes in Menasha, Wisconsin has permanently closed and is referring its bowlers to a nearby center in Appleton, marking another closure in the traditional bowling center segment. The closure reflects ongoing consolidation pressures facing independent bowling operators amid rising costs and shifting consumer entertainment habits. Multi-location operators may find opportunity in absorbing displaced customer bases from closed competitors.

articles.title: Industry Expert: Family Coasters Are Key to Theme Park and FEC Success
articles.summary: Industry voice Niles argues that family coasters represent one of the most reliable investments for driving repeat visitation and broadening demographic appeal at parks and large FEC venues. The argument points to family-oriented capital investments as a smarter long-term strategy than thrill-focused attractions. Larger FEC operators and park developers evaluating capital expenditure priorities will find this perspective relevant.

articles.title: Chamber Members Tour New Bear River Entertainment Zone in Northern California
articles.summary: Chamber of commerce members from Fortuna and Eureka got a preview tour of the new Bear River entertainment zone, signaling business community interest in the venue's economic impact. New entertainment zones developed in partnership with tribal gaming or resort entities are an emerging format in rural and semi-rural markets. FEC operators considering non-urban locations should watch how this venue performs as a destination draw.
